question_text: What is gas pressure caused by?

options.label: A
options.text: The collisions of gas particles with the walls of a container

options.label: B
options.text: The weight of the gas particles pushing downwards

options.label: C
options.text: The heat energy stored inside the gas

options.label: D
options.text: The density of the gas molecules packed together

explanation: Gas pressure is caused by gas particles moving randomly and colliding with the walls of their container. Each collision creates a tiny force, and millions of these collisions together produce the pressure we can measure.
